draftNote: Promoted from draft to owner-approved design authority by CBW-SITE-TEMPLATE-ARCHITECTURE-AUTHORITY-001-COMMIT.
ownerDecisions: Exchange-review template v2 approved (rectangular bottom logo, shared ExchangeOfferBlock, first-screen behavior, all three states, desktop and mobile layouts).

Project-wide rule: for every commercial page where a promo code and registration CTA are authorized by verified country-level evidence, the complete promo-code conversion surface must fit inside the first viewport on desktop and mobile.
The complete lower edge of the primary CTA must remain inside the viewport.
The rule applies only when the offer is eligible and verified for the relevant user or country context.
Never display a global promotion as locally eligible without country-level evidence.

terminology.firstScreen: Visible webpage viewport at page load, before any scroll.
terminology.primaryConversionSurface: H1/identity + status + verdict + complete code + complete Copy + complete registration/claim CTA + required notice.
terminology.complete: Full element box including bottom edge inside the viewport; partial visibility fails.
terminology.referralCompatibilityVerified: Evidence-backed boolean for country-context referral compatibility.
terminology.exchangeOfferBlock: Single shared conversion component (top FULL / bottom COMPACT).
terminology.rectangularWordmarkSlot: ExchangePromoLogoSlot system; wordmark never cropped square or stretched.

viewportMeasurement.definition: Visible webpage viewport excluding browser chrome.
rules: Prototype-only debug controls must not consume document-flow height.
Fixed or sticky UI must not cover required conversion elements.
Complete bottom edge of the primary CTA must remain visible.
Partially visible buttons do not pass.
Do not pass by shrinking text below readable sizes.
Do not pass by hiding required eligibility warnings.

mustContain: complete registration or Claim Bonus CTA
Check Eligibility or Visit Exchange action where permitted
explicit statement that local referral eligibility is not verified
View Available Alternatives action when alternatives exist
mustNotContain: claim of technical site inaccessibility unless separately verified

mustContain: Check Availability or View Verified Alternatives action where appropriate

perViewportPassConditions: complete promo code visible when state-required
complete primary CTA visible with bottom edge inside viewport
no vertical scrolling required to discover the CTA
header included in the height calculation
validationMatrix.prototypeProof: 24/24 pass (4 state configurations x 6 required viewports); worst margin CTA bottom 650px at 320x700

promoCodeLocallyEligibleOnlyWhenAll: the route and code are authorized
registrationOrClaimCtaOnlyWhenAll: no conflicting restriction blocks the action
neverShow: a global maximum as locally applicable without evidence
a promo code for a restricted exchange
a registration CTA for a restricted exchange
a claim CTA when referral compatibility is unverified
a global promotion as locally verified without evidence

usabilityRules: CTA touch target at least 44 CSS pixels high
Copy button touch target at least 44 CSS pixels high on mobile
no clipped limitation or restriction notice
readable text without artificial scale reduction
status must not rely on color alone
no oversized empty decorative area above the offer
no popup required to access the main offer
no obstructive sticky banner hiding page content
failureConditions: CTA bottom edge below the fold at any required viewport
state-required promo code or Copy control clipped or below the fold
required amber/red notice below the fold or hidden
required element covered by fixed/sticky UI
text shrunk below readable size to pass
promo/claim controls rendered in a state that forbids them
global offer presented as locally eligible without evidence

topBlock: may include richer verdict and trust information
bottomBlock: must not drift into a separate visual or factual system

implementationBoundaries: Design behavior authority only; no production routes, pages, components, data bindings, affiliate routes, sitemap, canonical or robots changes.
Prototypes: noindex/nofollow, sitemap-absent, mock data, no real affiliate navigation.
Production adoption requires separate owner migration authorization, verified data binding, production QA and deploy authorization.
Amendments require a new owner-approved version.
